ogTitle: Waddy Forest vs Wagerup: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Waddy Forest and Wagerup.
heading: Waddy Forest vs Wagerup — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Waddy Forest, Western Australia and Wagerup, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Waddy Forest has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Wagerup (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Waddy Forest or Wagerup safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Waddy Forest has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Wagerup scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Waddy Forest scores 15/100 for bushfire risk. Wagerup scores 37/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Waddy Forest or Wagerup?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Waddy Forest has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
